app.name: ProSSH
app.builtWith: Built with Tauri, Rust and React.
app.selectSession: Select a session or create a new one.

session.testOnlyPassword: Test connect currently only exercises password authentication. Key-based auth and passphrase prompt are wired via the terminal Connect button.
session.hostKeyTofu: Host key added on first use (TOFU)

dialog.name: Name
dialog.nameHostRequired: Name, host and username are required.
dialog.keepStoredSecret: Leave blank to keep stored secret
dialog.fillDetails: Fill in the connection details and save to add this session to ProSSH.
dialog.portForwardingDesc: Forward local or remote ports through the SSH tunnel. Rules activate on connect.

import.title: Import from SSH config
import.discovered: Discovered Host entries from ~/.ssh/config. Select the ones you want to import as sessions.
import.puttyDesc: Discovered PuTTY sessions from the Windows Registry.
import.puttyNotWindows: PuTTY import is only available on Windows.
import.mobaDesc: Import SSH sessions from the Windows Registry or a .mxtsessions file.
import.mobaNotWindows: MobaXTerm import is only available on Windows.

hostKey.title: Unknown host key
hostKey.description: The server at {{host}}:{{port}} presented a host key that is not in your known hosts. Verify the fingerprint below before accepting.
hostKey.trustNote: If you accept, this key will be saved to your known hosts and trusted for future connections to this server.

passphrase.title: Passphrase required
passphrase.description: The private key is encrypted. Enter the passphrase to unlock it.
passphrase.label: Passphrase

credentials.title: Credentials required
credentials.reasonNoPassword: No password is stored for this session. Enter credentials to connect.
credentials.reasonNoUsername: No username is configured. Enter credentials to connect.
credentials.reasonKeyRejected: The server rejected the SSH key. Enter a password to connect.

errors.noPassword: No password stored for this session

settings.title: Settings
settings.colorProfileDesc: Choose a terminal color scheme. The change applies to new terminals.
settings.terminalDesc: Mouse behaviour and clipboard settings. Changes apply to new terminals.
settings.rightClickPasteDesc: Paste clipboard contents into the terminal on right-click.
settings.copyOnSelectDesc: Automatically copy selected text to the clipboard.
settings.rememberSftpDirDesc: Restore the last visited directory when reconnecting to a session via SFTP.
settings.restoreSessionsDesc: Reopen terminal and SFTP tabs that were open when the app was closed.
settings.autoSftpTabDesc: Automatically switch the sidebar to the SFTP file browser when connecting to a session.
settings.monitoringDesc: Poll intervals for remote system metrics. Changes apply after switching tabs.
settings.fastIntervalDesc: How often to poll CPU, memory, swap, load averages and GPU metrics.
settings.slowIntervalDesc: How often to poll disk usage, OS info, hostname and uptime.
settings.scriptsDesc: Commands that appear in the command palette. Global scripts are available for all sessions.
settings.scriptsSessionDesc: Scripts specific to this session. They appear alongside global scripts in the command palette.
settings.shortcutsReadOnly: These shortcuts are read-only for now.

security.description: Choose where ProSSH stores session passwords and key passphrases. The encrypted file vault is a portable fallback for systems where the OS keyring isn't available.
security.keyringUnavailable: The OS keyring is not reachable on this system. Switch to the encrypted file vault below, or install a Secret Service provider (e.g. gnome-keyring).
security.backendAutoDesc: Use the OS keyring when it's available, fall back to the encrypted file vault otherwise.
security.backendKeyringDesc: Windows Credential Manager / macOS Keychain / Secret Service. Zero configuration.
security.backendFileDesc: A single file unlocked with a master password you choose. Works everywhere.
security.fileLocked: Encrypted vault is locked — unlock it to access stored secrets.
security.fileMissing: No encrypted vault yet — create one to get started.
security.destroyConfirm: Delete the encrypted vault? All stored passwords will be lost — you'll need to re-enter them on next connect.

sync.description: Move sessions, port forwards, color profiles, scripts and stored passwords between devices. Everything is encrypted with a passphrase you choose before it leaves your machine.
sync.fileSectionDesc: Export everything to a single encrypted file. Carry it via USB, email, Dropbox — whatever you already use. No setup, no accounts.
sync.driveSectionDesc: Sign in with Google to push and pull a single encrypted file from your Drive. Useful if you want one-click sync across devices.
sync.credentialsHelp: Override the built-in OAuth client with your own. Create an "OAuth 2.0 Client ID" of type "Desktop app" in Google Cloud Console. Leave Client ID blank to fall back to the built-in credentials.
sync.clientIdEmbeddedPlaceholder: Leave empty to use built-in credentials
sync.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect from Google Drive? Your local data stays put; cloud data is left alone.
sync.pullStats: Imported: {{sessions}} sessions, {{groups}} groups, {{portForwards}} port forwards, {{colorProfiles}} color profiles, {{scripts}} scripts, {{secrets}} secrets.
sync.dialogPushDesc: Choose a passphrase — the same one must be used to pull from another device. The payload is encrypted on this machine before it leaves.
sync.dialogPullDesc: Enter the passphrase you used when pushing. Matching entries on this device will be overwritten with the remote values.
sync.dialogExportDesc: Choose a passphrase — you'll need the same one to import on another device. The file is encrypted before being written to disk.
sync.dialogImportDesc: Enter the passphrase you used when exporting. Matching entries on this device will be overwritten with the imported values.
sync.warnPassphrase: If you forget this passphrase, the encrypted payload cannot be decrypted — treat it like the vault password.
sync.dialogSetupDesc: Choose a passphrase used to encrypt every snapshot pushed to Drive. The same passphrase must be set on your other devices to pull and decrypt.
sync.rememberHint: Stores the passphrase in the OS keyring so auto-sync survives a restart.
sync.passphraseMissing: Master passphrase is not set — auto-sync paused.

vault.descCreate: Pick a master password. ProSSH uses it to derive an encryption key for the vault. Don't lose it — there is no recovery.
vault.descUnlock: Enter the master password to decrypt session credentials.
vault.descChange: Re-encrypt the vault under a new master password.
vault.warnLoss: If you forget this password, the vault cannot be recovered — all stored secrets will be lost.

errors.noPassword: No password stored for this session
errors.noKey: No private key configured for this session

knownHosts.title: Known hosts
knownHosts.description: SSH host keys ProSSH trusts for each server. Revoke an entry to force-prompt on the next connect.
knownHosts.emptyHint: Entries are added automatically the first time you accept a server's key during connect.

files.noSession: Connect to a session to browse files.
files.scpNotFound: Neither rsync nor scp found on the source server.
files.scpInstallPrompt: Install on the source server? This will run: {{command}}
files.scpNoKey: No SSH key found on the source server.
files.scpGeneratePrompt: Generate a new SSH key (ed25519) on the source server?
